title: Walang Sugat
creator: Direction of: Enrique Herrera- Davila
description:
Walang Sugat, written by Severino Reyes, is one of the most important Filipino sarsuwela that bridges stage and cinema in Philippine cultural history. It was first performed in 1902 as a stage production at Teatro Libertad in Manila, with music by Fulgencio Tolentino. This original staging is highly significant as it emerged during the early post-revolutionary period, using theater as a form of resistance and national storytelling. The play portrayed themes of love, sacrifice, and struggle against Spanish colonial rule, and it quickly became one of the most influential works in Filipino theater.

Its legacy continued to grow as it transitioned into film, most notably with its first silver screen adaptation in 1939, directed by Enrique Herrera-Dávila. This early sound film version brought Walang Sugat to a wider audience during the pre-war Golden Age of Philippine cinema. Starring Leopoldo Salcedo and Rosa del Rosario, the 1939 adaptation preserved the emotional core of the original stage play while introducing cinematic storytelling techniques that helped define early Filipino filmmaking. Together, the 1902 stage performance and the 1939 film adaptation mark the work’s evolution from live theatrical tradition to enduring cinematic heritage.

Theater performance timeline
*1902 - FIrst performed in stage at the Teatro Libertad.
*1971 – First post-World War II revival staged at the Cultural Center of the Philippines (CCP). Dr. Herminio Velarde Jr. led the reconstruction of the lost original music through research and interviews, while Mike Velarde Jr. and Constancio de Guzman composed additional music still used in productions today.
*1991 – Staged by Tanghalang Pilipino, directed by Dennis Faustino with the Philippine Philharmonic Orchestra conducted by Josefino “Chino” Toledo. Later released on DVD as part of the CCP Sarswela Series.
*2009 – Produced by Barasoain Kalinangan Foundation Inc. during the UP Sarsuwela Festival at the University Theater, University of the Philippines.
*2010 – Tanghalang Ateneo staged its first major zarzuela production of Walang Sugat as part of Ateneo de Manila University’s Sesquicentennial Celebrations, directed by Ricardo Abad.
*2012 – Tanghalang Pilipino opened its 26th season with Walang Sugat at CCP’s Tanghalang Aurelio V. Tolentino, directed by Carlos Siguion-Reyna.
*2024 – Staged by the UP College of Music’s Nicanor Abelardo Hall Concert Series In Transit at the UP College of Architecture, featuring reconstructed music arranged by Josefino “Chino” Toledo.
subject: Theater to Film, Musical film, Film adaptation

title: Good News - Power (Radio Episode Excerpt)
creator: Robinson, Frank B.
description: Example locally hosted audio item. A short excerpt from a Psychiana radio program episode that begins with assurance that there is a Power that can get people what they desire. Robinson then discusses his early life failures and childhood seeking of some force that could turn his failures into success. He asserts the God-Power can turn our failures into success and bring us material and spiritual abundance.
subject: Frank B. Robinson; Failure; Success; Material Abundance
